name: Scottish Bap
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour and salt into a bowl.
2. Rub in the butter until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
3. Dissolve the yeast in a little of the warm water, then mix in more water and milk until smooth.
4. Add this to the dry ingredients gradually, mixing with your hands until a firm dough forms. If necessary, add a bit more flour so that the dough doesn't stick to the bowl.
5. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes (or until smooth and elastic).
6. Cover and let rise until doubled in size.
7. Turn out onto a floured surface and shape into a ball.
8. Roll out to a thickness of about 2 cm (1/4 inch) and transfer to a lightly buttered and floured baking tray.
9. Dredge the bap with plain flour on both sides.
10. Cover and let rise until doubled in size again.
11. Gently press your fingers into the surface of the bap three times to prevent blistering.
12. Bake in a moderately hot oven (200°C/400°F or Gas Mark 6) for 20 to 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
13. Cool on a wire rack.
